title: Question: Configure Problem could not create Makefile
body:
---


Author Name: **william huang**
Original Redmine Message: 1918 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I installed verilator on usr/local/bin.
However, I worked on a project that requires verilator, which required verilator_bin to be installed in a folder called "verilator"
Therefore, I delete everything related to verilator in bin. And plan to install again using ./configure --prefix=/opt/verilator
It could not create Makefile anymore. I had re-downloaded the verilator 3.884 or 3.882 several times, but still the same results.
Please advise. Thank you


```
~/verilator-3.882$ ./configure --prefix=/opt/verilator
configuring for Verilator 3.882 2016-03-01
checking whether to disable hardcoded paths... yes
checking whether to show and stop on compilation warnings... no

checking for g++... g++

checking whether g++ accepts -g... yes
checking for a BSD-compatible install... /usr/bin/install -c
checking that C++ compiler can compile simple program... yes
checking for perl... /usr/bin/perl
checking for flex... /usr/bin/flex
checking for bison... /usr/bin/bison
checking how to run the C++ preprocessor... g++ -E
checking for grep that handles long lines and -e... /bin/grep
checking for egrep... /bin/grep -E
checking for ANSI C header files... yes

checking whether g++ accepts -Qunused-arguments... no
checking whether g++ accepts -Wno-unused... yes
checking whether g++ accepts -Wno-parentheses-equality... yes
checking whether g++ accepts -Wno-null-conversion... yes

checking whether g++ accepts -Wno-sign-compare... yes
checking whether g++ accepts -Wno-uninitialized... yes

configure: creating ./config.status
config.status: creating Makefile
awk: symbol lookup error: awk: undefined symbol: mpfr_z_sub
config.status: error: could not create Makefile

```
